text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
(I was familiar with emptiness. Like a gaping hole it lurked 
within my heart, a void dark and endless.)

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
(As if yearning to fill up its depths, 
my heart's beat rushed me onward.)

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: (Even if its desire may never be fulfilled.)

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: (Even if its course may stretch forever.)

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: (Because that is all it ever knew.)

The "stars of the next generation" are all too common a topic 
among trainers. In recent times however, a particular name 
had begun standing out.

text_block_list.name: Older Trainer
text_block_list.text:
That's right. Quite something, she is. Hailed as a prodigy 
from a young age. I heard she never looked surprised when 
winning, even in the big tournaments. Like it was a given.

text_block_list.name: Similarly-Aged Trainer
text_block_list.text:
Not for nothing she's prospected to be a big name 
in the G1 classics, I see. Oh, how I wish I could be 
the trainer of someone like her…

I wanted to get a good look at this lauded prodigy, 
so I walked myself to the track. It looked like she 
was just about to start running. Lucky me.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: …Hooohh… haaah… …Hooohh…

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: Haahh…!

text_block_list.name: Cheerful Trainee
text_block_list.text:
…Look at her go! Hmm… you know, something about 
the way she runs is just different from the rest of us…
choice_data_list: (I can tell immediately. It's her form…!)

text_block_list.text:
It's beautiful. Devoid of nonnecessities. Every action perfectly 
converted into forward motion. All those present were rooted 
to the ground in awe.

Even among the higher levels of racers it would be difficult 
to find someone who not only understood the elements of 
form this well, but could effectively realize them so perfectly.
And this before her debut. She must be driven by a fierce rivalry, surrounded by peers of equal ambition.
choice_data_list: (Yet, as this thought entered my mind…)

text_block_list.name: Cheerful Trainee
text_block_list.text:
U-Umm… Arve! I'm… I'm aiming to debut 
this year as well, and…!

text_block_list.name: Cheerful Trainee
text_block_list.text:
I really want to learn about your form and how you can 
be so fast! Would you mind training together with me…?!

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
…I don't intend to train with anyone. You're asking 
the wrong person, try someone else.

The girl's earnest request was coldly pushed aside. 
Indifferent to the resulting mood shift among onlookers, 
she began running — alone.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: Hraaaaah…!

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: Haah… haa… haaah… haah…! Still… three to go… urk.

text_block_list.name: Air Groove
text_block_list.text: Hm…? Admire Groove…? Do you feel alright?
Air Groove had appeared and ran over to the staggering Admire Groove. She was a top-class trainee with a spectacular record, even for Central standards.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: Hahh… hah…… I'm fine.

text_block_list.name: Air Groove
text_block_list.text: Hmph. You never fail to impress with your determination and discipline. However, had you the company of others, you could still learn from observing them while taking a rest.

text_block_list.name: Air Groove
text_block_list.text:
Why not join us this once? There's nothing wrong with 
working on things alone, but learning in group is valuable 
in its own ——

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
…You needn't concern yourself with me. 
I have my own ways of training.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
Growing stronger is… something I alone am responsible for. 
…I have a training program to finish, so if you'll excuse me…

Even Air Groove's words seemed to fall on deaf ears. 
I guess you could call this an example of talking to a wall.

After finishing training, Admire Groove returns trackside. 
Perhaps she'll just think me meddlesome, but…

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
…That badge means you're a trainer. 
Did you need something?
choice_data_list: I saw Air Groove talking to you earlier.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
…It wasn't because I caused any issues. She invited me 
to train with her, and I declined. That's all.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: I find that sort of attention bothersome. Surely she can't have intended to approach me when coming here, either.
choice_data_list: I think she was just worried about you.

text_block_list.name: Orphanage Worker
text_block_list.text:
You're great at both studying and gym time, and just about 
anything else, huh? That's really amazing. The other teachers 
think so too, you know — we all see a bright future for you!

text_block_list.name: Young Arve
text_block_list.text: Yeah. I'll try hard at studying and running.

text_block_list.name: Orphanage Director
text_block_list.text:
To think the day would come when we could see you off 
to Tracen Academy… I'm confident you'll do just fine. 
You've always been a capable kid.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
…I have attained the necessary skills and knowledge while here. 
My results in the kids' races were strong, too. So…

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: There is no need for that.
Her stare was cold as ice.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
So-called bonds and friendship… I have no need of those. 
They are irrelevant to my personal growth.

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text:
Much less feelings of affection… worthless distractions. 
So I ask you…

text_block_list.name: Admire Groove
text_block_list.text: …not to speak as if you know me.

My first conversation with the prodigy named Admire Groove 
felt much like plunging into a frozen lake.
